footer.tagline: Open-source AI agent SDK and CLI. AGPL-3.0, with a commercial license available.

hero.title: The open-source AI agent SDK
hero.description: Multi-provider CLI (bring your own key) and embeddable SDK. Switch between

features.title: Bring Your Own Key — No Subscription
features.description: Use your own API key and pay only for the tokens you use. No monthly plans, no per-seat pricing.

features.title: Any Provider, One Config
features.description: Switch between Anthropic, OpenAI, DeepSeek, Gemini, or a local Ollama model by changing one line in your config.

features.title: Embeddable SDK
features.description: Import @robota-sdk/agent-framework and embed the same agent runtime that powers the CLI.

features.title: Self-Hostable & Auditable
features.description: Run on-premises with local LLMs — prompts and data stay in your own infrastructure. The full source is public and auditable (AGPL-3.0, with a commercial license for closed-source use).

features.title: TypeScript-First
features.description: Strict types end-to-end — no any, no implicit casts, no unexpected runtime behavior. Built for real TypeScript codebases.

cta.title: Get started in minutes
cta.description: No account. No subscription. Just a terminal and an API key.
cta.readGuide: Read the full Getting Started guide →

beta.title: Join the Robota Beta
beta.description: Robota is in active beta. Try the CLI and SDK today, and send feedback that informs what we build next.
beta.comingSoonBody: We're putting the finishing touches on beta onboarding. In the meantime, install the CLI from the docs or share feedback on GitHub.

compare.title: Why Robota?
compare.description: A multi-provider AI coding CLI that lets you bring your own key for any provider, run fully offline with a local model, and embed the same engine into your own app. Source-available and self-hostable, with both an open-source (AGPL-3.0) and a commercial license.

items.title: 1. Any Provider — One Config
items.body: Switch between Anthropic, OpenAI, DeepSeek, Gemini, or Ollama by changing one line in ~/.robota/settings.json. No code changes, no new subscriptions.

items.title: 2. Embeddable SDK
items.body: Ship the same agent runtime to your users via @robota-sdk/agent-framework. Few AI coding assistants ship their runtime as an embeddable library — most (Claude Code, Cursor, Cline) are delivered only as end-user products.

items.title: 3. Open & Self-Hostable
items.body: Every line is publicly auditable — fork it, modify it, and self-host with no vendor lock-in. Open-source use is AGPL-3.0; a commercial license is available for proprietary or SaaS use.

items.title: 4. Local Model First-Class
items.body: Point any Ollama or LM Studio model as your provider. When you use a local model, your code and prompts stay on your machine.

items.label: Choose Claude Code
items.body: if you want the tightest Claude integration and are happy with Anthropic-only.

items.label: Choose Cursor
items.body: if you want an IDE-first experience with inline diff editing and tab completion.

items.label: Choose Aider
items.body: if you prefer a Python ecosystem and work primarily with git-based batch commits.

items.label: Choose Cline
items.body: if you want a VSCode sidebar agent and don't need embedding or SDK usage.

enterprise.title: Enterprise
enterprise.description: Robota is used by engineering teams that need a controllable, self-hostable AI coding assistant. This page covers security practices, deployment options, and how to get in touch.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.description: To discuss team licensing, on-premises deployment, priority support, or custom integrations, open a GitHub Discussion or email us directly.
contact.responseTime: We aim to respond to enterprise inquiries within

dataHandling.title: Data Handling
dataHandling.description: Robota operates as a local CLI or self-hosted server.
dataHandling.highlight: No conversation data is stored or transmitted to Robota servers
dataHandling.descriptionSuffix: — the SDK calls the AI provider of your choice directly from your machine or infrastructure.
rows: Sent only to the AI provider you configure
Stored in your local environment variables or secrets manager

onPremises.title: On-Premises Deployment
onPremises.description: Robota can run in air-gapped environments with local LLMs (install packages from an internal mirror):

items.name: Ollama
items.body: run models locally with zero external network calls

items.name: Any OpenAI-compatible endpoint
items.body: point baseURL to your internal gateway

highlights.title: Auditable source code
highlights.body: The full source is public on GitHub for security review (github.com/woojubb/robota)

highlights.title: No telemetry
highlights.body: No analytics, no phone-home in the SDK or CLI

highlights.title: Append-only session logs
highlights.body: You control retention and deletion of all local session files

highlights.title: Stays inside your compliance boundary
highlights.body: Robota stores no data of its own, so your existing controls and your provider's certifications (e.g. SOC 2, ISO 27001) remain the system of record

items.q: Does Robota store my code in the cloud?
items.a: No. All file reads and writes happen on your local machine. The only data that leaves your machine is the prompt you send to your configured AI provider.

items.q: Can we use Robota behind a corporate proxy?
items.a: Yes. Set the standard HTTPS_PROXY environment variable and the SDK's HTTP client will route through it.

items.q: Can Robota be installed in a restricted network with no internet access?
items.a: Yes — use a local LLM (Ollama, LM Studio) and install npm packages from an internal registry mirror.

items.q: Is there a commercial license option?
items.a: Yes. Robota is dual-licensed: free under the AGPL-3.0, with a commercial license available for proprietary or closed-source use that cannot meet the AGPL-3.0 obligations. Enterprise support contracts (SLA, dedicated channels, custom integrations) are also available — contact us for details.
